content.text:
Parallel and Distributed Algorithmics
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEM2  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lecture:
1h30  |
Tutorial:
1h30  |
Lab:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Familiarise students with the concepts, techniques, and tools of parallel and distributed algorithmics. Students will be able to understand parallel and distributed system architectures, design parallel and distributed algorithms, understand the complexity of parallel and distributed algorithmics, and master parallel and distributed programming techniques.
Prerequisites:
Fundamentals of algorithmics and algorithm complexity
Programming in C or Python
Computer system architectures and operating systems
Computer networks and communication protocols
Course Content (21h)
Chapter 1 – Foundations
Parallel computation models (PRAM, BSP)
Performance measures (Amdahl's law, Gustafson)
Parallelism patterns
Chapter 2 – Basic Algorithms
Parallel sorting and searching
Prefix-sum, parallel matrix products
FFT
Chapter 3 – Graph Problems and Optimisation
Shortest paths (Floyd-Warshall, Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford)
Parallel heuristics (genetic, ant colony)
Parallel neural networks
Chapter 4 – Parallel Programming
OpenMP, native threads, GPU
Synchronisation (barriers, locks, atomics)
Profiling and debugging
Chapter 5 – Distributed Computing
Architectures (clusters, grids, cloud)
MPI, MapReduce, Spark
Consistency, fault tolerance (consensus, checkpointing)
Chapter 6 – Distributed System Architecture
Communication protocols (TCP/IP, RDMA, gRPC)
Load balancing, high availability
Security and isolation
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — written tests, personal work
Bibliography
Bertsekas, D. P., & Tsitsiklis, J. N.
Parallel and Distributed Computation: Numerical Methods
.
Wilkinson, B., & Allen, M.
Parallel Programming: Techniques and Applications Using Networked Workstations and Parallel Computers
.
Kshemkalyani, A. D., & Singhal, M.
Distributed Computing: Principles, Algorithms, and Systems
.
Grama, A., Karypis, G., Kumar, V., & Gupta, A.
Introduction to Parallel Computing
.
